name: context-density
description: Context design, prompt-contract optimization, research-backed acceptance gates, and compression validation for agent skills, plugins, prompts, docs, and workflows.

interface.shortDescription: Measure context cost, validate compression, and enforce explicit LLM output contracts.
interface.longDescription: Context Density turns token efficiency, long-context placement, retrieval provenance, prompt-cache layout, and prompt-contract discipline into one agent workflow: identify the consumer and load path, measure context cost, remove drift and duplication, preserve behavioral invariants, enforce structured output contracts, apply research-backed acceptance gates, and validate the result with deterministic audits. When token pressure exposes skill/plugin split, merge, move, delete, router, or cross-plugin overlap decisions, it routes structural work to Capability Workbench portfolio architecture.
capabilities: Research-backed acceptance gates for long-context placement, compression break-even, schema/task validity, retrieval citation promotion, and cache-prefix economics
Structural handoff to Capability Workbench for skill/plugin portfolio refactors
defaultPrompt: Use Context Density to audit this skill, prompt, or AGENTS.md for token cost, duplication, and prompt-contract risk.
Use Context Density to refactor this skill/plugin for lower hot-path token cost while preserving behavior, and route structural portfolio decisions to Capability Workbench.
Use Context Density to check long-context placement, retrieval provenance, cache-prefix economics, and compression quality before replacing raw context.
Use Context Density to review this LLM workflow for explicit JSON/schema/tool-call contracts and brittle prose parsing.
